# FinCore Engine — Full API Reference

FinCore is a double-entry accounting engine. It enforces that every journal entry's total debits equal total credits. It supports multi-tenant operation (each "client" is an isolated tenant with its own chart of accounts and journal entries).

## Base URLs

| Environment | URL |
|-------------|-----|
| Production  | https://fincore-engine.fly.dev |
| Staging     | https://fincore-staging.fly.dev |
| Local dev   | http://localhost:5002 |

## Rate Limits

- 100 requests per minute per IP address (unauthenticated and authenticated)
- 50 requests per second per authenticated client (burst: 100)

---

## Authentication

FinCore uses header-based API key authentication.

### Step 1: Register a client (no auth required)

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "My App", "email": "myapp@example.com"}'
```

Response:
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "7e2e0544-931c-4c07-a761-5ae95202d4e1",
    "name": "My App",
    "email": "myapp@example.com",
    "client_id": "c_2d521294-a817-405d-b2a9-7580af3c774b",
    "client_secret": "p68XtVzrZKkOrKB1gW8kAkUeeXxxEzHxwbsqEgcvJEY",
    "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Important:** `client_secret` is only returned once at registration. Store it securely.

### Step 2: Authenticate all subsequent requests

Include both headers on every request:

```
X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_2d521294-a817-405d-b2a9-7580af3c774b
X-FinCore-Client-Secret: p68XtVzrZKkOrKB1gW8kAkUeeXxxEzHxwbsqEgcvJEY
```

```sh
curl https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/clients/me \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_2d521294-..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: p68XtVzr..."
```

---

## Response Patterns

### Single resource
```json
{ "data": { ...resource fields... } }
```

### List resource
```json
{
  "data": [ ...array of resources... ],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "page_size": 10,
    "order": "desc",
    "order_by": "created_at",
    "total": 42,
    "has_next_page": true,
    "has_previous_page": false
  }
}
```

### Error
```json
{ "errors": { "message": "description of the error" } }
```

### List query parameters (all list endpoints)

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | 1 | Page number |
| `page_size` | integer | 10 | Items per page |
| `order` | string | `desc` | Sort direction: `asc` or `desc` |
| `order_by` | string | `created_at` | Field to sort by |
| `query` | string | — | Search term |
| `search_fields` | string | — | Comma-separated fields to search |
| `start_date` | date | — | Filter by created_at >= start_date |
| `end_date` | date | — | Filter by created_at <= end_date |
| `populate` | string | — | Comma-separated relations to eager-load |

---

## Clients API

### POST /api/v1/clients — Register a new client
No authentication required. Creates a new isolated tenant.

**Request body:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | Client name (3–255 chars) |
| `email` | string | Yes | Client email address (unique) |

**Response:** `201 Created` — returns the client object with `client_id` and `client_secret`.

---

### GET /api/v1/clients/me — Get current client
Returns the authenticated client's details. `client_secret` is not returned after initial registration.

**Response:** `200 OK`
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "name": "My App",
    "email": "myapp@example.com",
    "client_id": "c_...",
    "client_secret": null,
    "created_at": "...",
    "updated_at": "..."
  }
}
```

---

## Accounts API

Accounts form a chart of accounts for your client. They support a hierarchy (group accounts contain child accounts) and five standard accounting types.

### Account types
- `ASSET` — Resources owned (cash, receivables, inventory)
- `LIABILITY` — Obligations owed (payables, loans)
- `EQUITY` — Owner's equity (capital, retained earnings)
- `INCOME` — Revenue earned (sales, interest income)
- `EXPENSE` — Costs incurred (rent, salaries, cost of goods)

### POST /api/v1/accounts — Create an account

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/accounts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: ..." \
  -d '{
    "name": "Cash",
    "type": "ASSET",
    "is_contra": false,
    "is_group": false
  }'
```

**Request body:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | Account name (3–255 chars) |
| `type` | enum | Yes | One of: ASSET, LIABILITY, EQUITY, INCOME, EXPENSE |
| `is_contra` | boolean | Yes | True if this is a contra account (offsets its parent type) |
| `is_group` | boolean | Yes | True if this account is a group/parent (cannot have journal entry lines) |
| `parent_account_id` | UUID | No | ID of a parent group account |
| `description` | string | No | Account description (3–255 chars) |

**Response:** `201 Created`
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "code": "1001",
    "name": "Cash",
    "type": "ASSET",
    "is_contra": false,
    "is_group": false,
    "description": null,
    "parent_account_id": null,
    "parent_account": null,
    "created_at": "...",
    "updated_at": "..."
  }
}
```

---

### GET /api/v1/accounts — List accounts

Additional filters beyond the standard list params:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `account_type` | enum | Filter by account type |
| `is_contra` | boolean | Filter contra/non-contra accounts |
| `is_group` | boolean | Filter group/non-group accounts |
| `parent_account_id` | UUID | Filter by parent account |
| `populate` | string | Use `ParentAccount` to include parent object |

---

### GET /api/v1/accounts/{account_id} — Get single account

```sh
curl https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/accounts/uuid \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: ..."
```

---

### PATCH /api/v1/accounts/{account_id} — Update an account
Only name and description can be updated after creation.

**Request body (all fields optional):**
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | New account name (3–255 chars) |
| `description` | string | New description (3–255 chars) |

---

### DELETE /api/v1/accounts/{account_id} — Delete an account

Constraints:
- Cannot delete a group account that still has child accounts
- Cannot delete an account that has journal entry lines

**Response:** `204 No Content`

---

## Journal Entries API

Journal entries are the core of double-entry bookkeeping. Each entry contains 2+ lines, and **the sum of all debit amounts must equal the sum of all credit amounts**.

### Journal entry lifecycle

```
DRAFT  →  POSTED
```

- `DRAFT`: Can be edited or deleted. Not yet finalized.
- `POSTED`: Finalized and immutable. Cannot be deleted.

To post a draft: `PATCH /api/v1/journal-entries/{id}/post`

---

### POST /api/v1/journal-entries — Create a journal entry

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/journal-entries \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: ..." \
  -d '{
    "status": "DRAFT",
    "reference": "INV-001",
    "transaction_date": "2024-01-15",
    "lines": [
      { "account_id": "cash-account-uuid", "debit": 50000, "credit": 0, "notes": "Cash received" },
      { "account_id": "revenue-account-uuid", "debit": 0, "credit": 50000, "notes": "Sales revenue" }
    ]
  }'
```

**Request body:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `status` | enum | Yes | `DRAFT` or `POSTED` |
| `reference` | string | Yes | Unique reference (e.g. invoice number, 3–255 chars) |
| `transaction_date` | date | No | ISO 8601 date (e.g. `2024-01-15`) |
| `metadata` | object | No | Arbitrary JSON for your own use |
| `lines` | array | Yes | Minimum 2 lines; total debits must equal total credits |

**Line fields:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `account_id` | UUID | Yes | The account this line posts to |
| `debit` | number | Yes | Debit amount (integer cents/smallest unit; >= 0) |
| `credit` | number | Yes | Credit amount (integer cents/smallest unit; >= 0) |
| `notes` | string | No | Line-level notes (3–255 chars) |

**Response:** `201 Created` — returns the full journal entry with lines.

---

### GET /api/v1/journal-entries — List journal entries

Additional filters:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `status` | enum | Filter by `DRAFT` or `POSTED` |
| `populate` | string | Use `JournalEntryLines` and/or `Account` |

---

### GET /api/v1/journal-entries/{journal_entry_id} — Get single entry

```sh
curl "https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/journal-entries/uuid?populate=JournalEntryLines" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: ..."
```

---

### PATCH /api/v1/journal-entries/{journal_entry_id} — Update a draft entry
Only DRAFT entries can be updated.

**Request body (all fields optional):**
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `reference` | string | New reference (3–255 chars) |
| `transaction_date` | date | New transaction date |
| `metadata` | object | New metadata |
| `lines` | array | Replace all lines (min 2; debits must equal credits) |

**Update line fields (all optional):**
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | UUID | Line ID to update an existing line; omit to add a new line |
| `account_id` | UUID | Account for this line |
| `debit` | number | New debit amount |
| `credit` | number | New credit amount |
| `notes` | string | New notes |

---

### PATCH /api/v1/journal-entries/{journal_entry_id}/post — Post a draft entry
Finalizes the entry. No request body needed. Only DRAFT entries can be posted.

```sh
curl -X PATCH https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/journal-entries/uuid/post \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: c_..." \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: ..."
```

**Response:** `200 OK` — returns the updated entry with `status: "POSTED"` and `posted_at` set.

---

### DELETE /api/v1/journal-entries/{journal_entry_id} — Delete a draft entry
Only DRAFT entries can be deleted.

**Response:** `204 No Content`

---

## Workflow: Recording a Sale

This end-to-end example walks through registering, creating accounts, recording a sale as a journal entry, and posting it.

### 1. Register your client

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Acme Corp", "email": "acme@example.com"}'

# Save the returned client_id and client_secret
export CLIENT_ID="c_2d521294-..."
export CLIENT_SECRET="p68XtVzr..."
```

### 2. Create a Cash account (ASSET)

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/accounts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: $CLIENT_ID" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: $CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -d '{"name": "Cash", "type": "ASSET", "is_contra": false, "is_group": false}'

# Save the returned id
export CASH_ACCOUNT_ID="uuid-of-cash-account"
```

### 3. Create a Sales Revenue account (INCOME)

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/accounts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: $CLIENT_ID" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: $CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -d '{"name": "Sales Revenue", "type": "INCOME", "is_contra": false, "is_group": false}'

export REVENUE_ACCOUNT_ID="uuid-of-revenue-account"
```

### 4. Record the sale as a DRAFT journal entry

A $500 cash sale: debit Cash (asset increases), credit Sales Revenue (income increases).

```sh
curl -X POST https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/journal-entries \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: $CLIENT_ID" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: $CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -d "{
    \"status\": \"DRAFT\",
    \"reference\": \"SALE-001\",
    \"transaction_date\": \"2024-01-15\",
    \"metadata\": {\"customer\": \"John Doe\", \"invoice\": \"INV-001\"},
    \"lines\": [
      {\"account_id\": \"$CASH_ACCOUNT_ID\", \"debit\": 50000, \"credit\": 0, \"notes\": \"Cash from sale\"},
      {\"account_id\": \"$REVENUE_ACCOUNT_ID\", \"debit\": 0, \"credit\": 50000, \"notes\": \"Sale of goods\"}
    ]
  }"

export JOURNAL_ENTRY_ID="uuid-of-journal-entry"
```

Note: amounts are in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents). 50000 = $500.00.

### 5. Post the journal entry

```sh
curl -X PATCH https://fincore-engine.fly.dev/api/v1/journal-entries/$JOURNAL_ENTRY_ID/post \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Id: $CLIENT_ID" \
  -H "X-FinCore-Client-Secret: $CLIENT_SECRET"
```

The entry is now `POSTED` and immutable.

---

## Double-Entry Rules

- Every journal entry must have at least 2 lines
- Sum of all `debit` values across all lines must equal sum of all `credit` values
- Each line must have exactly one of debit or credit as non-zero (though both can be provided)
- Amounts are integers (whole numbers) representing the smallest currency unit

## Common Errors

| HTTP Status | Meaning |
|-------------|---------|
| 400 | Bad request — see `errors.message` for details |
| 401 | Unauthorized — missing or invalid auth headers |
| 422 | Validation error — field-level validation failed |
| 500 | Internal server error |
